groups.text: It is currently 14 degrees and cloudy in Berlin-Tempelhof.
With the morning weather set, here is the news.

Google DeepMind has released DiffusionGemma, an experimental open model designed for exceptionally fast text generation. The new model delivers up to four times faster inference on dedicated GPUs. NVIDIA has already optimized the model to run across its GeForce RTX GPUs, the RTX PRO platform, and DGX Spark systems.

This push for local AI speed arrives as international tensions surface ahead of the 2026 World Cup. A viral video showing Senegal's national team being searched with metal detectors on a US airport tarmac has triggered backlash. Some social media users have described the incident as a racist humiliation of the players upon their entry into the United States.

While the tournament creates diplomatic friction, it also highlights specific economic drivers. In Germany, a shoe factory in the small town of Scheinfeld has become a key supplier for the event. One in three players at the World Cup in America will wear boots from this Franconian town, including national player Florian Wirtz.

Beyond the sports industry, broader analysis of American capitalism suggests that millionaires, rather than billionaires, hold the primary levers of power. These individuals often wield enormous influence while remaining less visible in the public eye.
After the headlines, back to the weather.
This afternoon will be cloudy with rain chances up to 58 percent and temperatures between 18 and 20 degrees.
And that's it for your personal morning broadcast.
[mature adult voice, intensely emotional, tender, radiant; hold a long breath after Rise] Rise... and shine.
[very very long pause before speaking; then mature adult voice, most emotional, strong, warm, radiant, deeply encouraging; hold a long breath after Rise] Rise... and shine.
